title: From Component Library to Product Speed
content: A component library is successful only if it shortens delivery time for product teams. If it adds process and complexity, teams will bypass it and create local alternatives.
My rule is simple: every component must reduce duplicated work in at least two product surfaces. If that signal is not there, it belongs in the product codebase, not in the shared library.
The second rule is contract stability. APIs and slots should be boring and explicit. The best platform components are predictable and easy to document.

Finally, optimize for contribution flow. A shared library should make it easy for product engineers to submit improvements. Fast review cycles and clear ownership are more important than perfect abstraction.
